channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Man jailed over police AI program, then freed 17 months after victim raised doubts
item.description: Chris Gatlin spent 17 months in jail for a crime that an artificial intelligence program said he committed, only to be freed after the prosecutor learned there was no real evidence.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, KTLA 5

item.title: McConnell slips and falls outside Senate Chamber
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) slipped and fell on the stairs leaving the Senate Chamber on Wednesday afternoon, prompting concerned aides to rush to help the 82-year-old lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Delphi murders: State's filing includes photos of Libby German's iPhone 6s
item.description: In November, a jury convicted Allen of murder in the February 2017 deaths of Abby Williams and Libby German near the Monon High Bridge.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Artist explains how New Orleans childhood inspired Super Bowl logo
item.description: Months before the Chiefs and Eagles earned a ticket to Super Bowl LIX, the NFL design team asked Tajh to create both theme art and the logo for Super Bowl 59.
item.dc:creator: Amy Russo, NewsNation

item.title: Jeremiah Trotter Jr. continues legacy of Eagles Super Bowl linebackers
item.description: His dad, known as the "Ax Man" for his signature sack celebration, played eight seasons in Midnight Green and is a member of the team's Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, NewsNation

item.title: Oklahoma board denies clemency for man on death row, clears path for March execution
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — An Oklahoma board rejected clemency on Wednesday for a man sentenced to die for fatally shooting a woman during a 2005 home-invasion robbery, clearing the way for him to become the first person executed in the state this year.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Black church in DC that was vandalized by the Proud Boys gains control over the group's trademark
item.description: The Proud Boys has lost the trademark to their name and logos after a judge gave the rights to an historic Black church in Washington that was vandalized by the far-right group's members in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: San Antonio Zoo helping people 'ex-terminate' their exes on Valentine's Day
item.description: The San Antonio Zoo has six festive events this Valentine's Day for those in love, falling out of love or seeking their next match.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Thompson, NewsNation

item.title: Rare invasive bug intercepted at Detroit airport
item.description: Agriculture specialists with U.S. Customs and Border Protection confirm that rare invasive seed bugs were intercepted on undeclared cargo late last year at Detroit Metro Airport.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KRON4

item.title: Authorities nab White House fence climber just two weeks into Trump's new term
item.description: U.S. Secret Service took someone into custody after the individual sought to scale the White House fence on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas man set to be executed for killing a pastor in his own church
item.description: A Texas man convicted of beating and suffocating a Dallas area pastor in his own church during a robbery was facing execution on Wednesday, the second scheduled execution in the U.S. so far this year. Steven Lawayne Nelson was condemned for the 2011 killing of the Rev. Clint Dobson.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Crews save unresponsive man in spinning boat on Florida lake
item.description: A man found unresponsive inside a boat that was driving in circles was saved by Florida authorities on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, KRON4

item.title: Armed teen arrested at Arkansas airport after allegedly demanding plane
item.description: After the employees got to safety, the suspect "breached through a door onto the airfield," an airport official said.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Trombetta, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect arrested after one dead, 5 injured in New Albany mass shooting
item.description: For the latest updates in New Albany warehouse shooting, follow this link. NEW ALBANY, Ohio (WCMH) -- A man is in custody after one person was shot dead and five others were injured after a shooting Tuesday night at a New Albany warehouse. According to authorities, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Mills, ABC 4

item.title: Racial gap widened in deaths among US moms around the time of childbirth
item.description: Black women in the U.S. died at a rate nearly 3.5 times higher than white women around the time of childbirth in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump will sign an executive order barring transgender female athletes from competing
item.description: President Donald Trump will sign an executive order on Wednesday designed to prevent people who were biologically assigned male at birth from participating in women’s or girls' sporting events.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk shocks lawmakers, setting himself on collision course
item.description: Billionaire businessman Elon Musk is on a collision course with lawmakers on Capitol Hill who are starting to challenge his authority.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Protests against Trump, Project 2025 planned in cities across the country
item.description: A movement to oppose the early actions of President Donald Trump’s administration is taking off online, with plans to protest across the U.S. on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Decorated pilot Harry Stewart, Jr., one of the last surviving Tuskegee Airmen, dies at 100
item.description: Retired Lt. Col. Harry Stewart Jr, a decorated World War II pilot who broke racial barriers as a Tuskegee Airmen and earned honors for his combat heroism, has died. He was 100. Stewart was one of the last surviving combat pilots of the famed 332nd Fighter Group also known as the Tuskegee Airmen.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US Postal Service flip-flops on Hong Kong-China packages, lifting a ban imposed a day earlier
item.description: Americans are likely to pay more for products from popular Chinese e-commerce platforms like Shein and Temu as the U.S. Postal Service said it would stop accepting parcels from China and Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump’s birthright citizenship order is put on hold by a second federal judge
item.description: A federal judge in Maryland has ordered a second nationwide pause on President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship for anyone born in the U.S. to someone in the country illegally.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman And Mike Catalini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration will consider redrawing boundaries of national monuments as part of energy push
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As part of the Trump administration’s push to expand U.S. energy production, federal officials will review and consider redrawing the boundaries of national monuments created under previous presidents to protect unique landscapes and cultural resources.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims in deadly DC plane and chopper collision now recovered
item.description: The chief medical examiner is still trying to positively identify one set of remains, officials said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Capri Suns are now available in reusable bottles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) – Capri Sun, the beverage company famous for its juice pouches, will soon be available in reusable bottles, marking the first time the brand is expanding its single-serve offerings in two decades.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Officials confirm all 67 victims in DC midair crash recovered from Potomac River
item.description: Officials confirmed Tuesday that all 67 victims in the DC midair crash have been recovered from the Potomac River, with 66 bodies positively identified.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Designs unveiled for replacement of Baltimore bridge almost a year after deadly collapse
item.description: SPARROWS POINT, Md. (AP) — Almost a year after the deadly collapse of Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge, Maryland officials on Tuesday unveiled their designs for its replacement, which will be taller and better protected against ship strikes.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order Wednesday banning transgender athletes from competing in girls’ and women’s sports, the latest in a string of executive actions from the new administration to take aim at LGBTQ rights.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: The Oregon Zoo welcomes a new baby elephant
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — The Oregon Zoo in Portland has welcomed its newest addition, a baby elephant. Thirty-year-old Asian elephant Rose-Tu gave birth on Saturday after 20 months of pregnancy, the zoo said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gerber recalls all batches of Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks nationwide
item.description: Gerber Products Company has announced a nationwide recall and discontinuation of all batches of its Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks due to a potential choking hazard for infants and young children.
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, KTLA 5

item.title: New York shields abortion pill prescribers after a doctor was indicted in Louisiana
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York Gov. Kathy Hochul on Monday signed a bill to shield the identities of doctors who prescribe abortion medications, days after a physician in the state was charged with prescribing abortion pills to a pregnant minor in Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Multiple Canadian provinces pull US-made booze over Trump tariffs
item.description: Nova Scotia Premier Tim Houston said the Nova Scotia Liquor Corporation will remove all U.S. alcohol from store shelves starting Tuesday, totaling more than 400 products, including beer, wine, spirits and coolers.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Lindsey Vonn faces more criticism for competing at age 40 than Tom Brady, Lewis Hamilton
item.description: Why did Lindsey Vonn face so much second guessing in Europe about her comeback at age 40 this season after spending the same time away from the sport as Hirscher?
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DAMPF, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Seven dead, 19 injured in 'high-impact' air ambulance crash in Philadelphia
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — A person in a car was the seventh fatal victim of the fiery crash of an air ambulance onto a busy Philadelphia street, authorities said Saturday, as investigators sifted through burned cars, damaged homes and charred debris for clues to determine why the aircraft plummeted shortl
item.dc:creator: MATT ROURKE and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4
